title: NOC 32122 – Medical Sonographers | GTR Immigration
description: Medical Sonographers under NOC 32122 are highly skilled diagnostic imaging professionals who operate ultrasound technology to support physicians in diagnosing and monitoring medical conditions. Across Canada, the demand for diagnostic imaging services continues to rise due to population growth, aging demographics, and expanding prenatal and cardiac care programs. As a TEER 2 occupation, Medical Sonographers qualify under Express Entry and various Provincial Nominee Programs, offering a structured pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3212 – Medical technologists and techniciansNOC Code32122

duties: Medical Sonographers perform some or all of the following duties:
Operate ultrasound imaging equipment to produce diagnostic images
Monitor image quality and adjust equipment as needed
Record, store, and process scanned images
Observe and care for patients during examinations
Assist in monitoring pregnancies and diagnosing cardiac, vascular, abdominal, and other disorders
Perform quality control checks and minor equipment maintenance
Supervise and train students or junior technologists
employment_requirements: Completion of:A three- to four-year program in diagnostic medical sonographyORA two- to three-year allied health diploma plus a one-year accredited post-diploma sonography program
A three- to four-year program in diagnostic medical sonographyOR
A two- to three-year allied health diploma plus a one-year accredited post-diploma sonography program
Registration with Sonography Canada is usually required
Registration with the American Registry of Diagnostic Medical Sonographers may be accepted by some employers
Registration with the College of Medical Radiation Technologists of Ontario is required in Ontario
Registration with Ordre des technologues en imagerie médicale, en radio-oncologie et en électrophysiologie médicale du Québec is required in Quebec
Internationally educated applicants must complete credential recognition and certification before practicing.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $50,900 to $102,000, depending on specialization and province.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 32122 is strong and positive, driven by:
Specializations in cardiac and vascular sonography may provide stronger employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: This positive gap indicates strong demand, particularly in provinces such as Alberta, Saskatchewan, British Columbia, and Ontario.
Why Immigrate to Canada as a Medical Sonographer?
Stable public and private sector employment
Direct PR opportunities under healthcare streams
Ultrasound imaging remains one of the most widely used diagnostic tools in Canadian healthcare.
pathways: If you are a qualified Medical Sonographer, NOC 32122 may provide a pathway toward permanent residence in Canada.
